profile.baseline_summary: Marcus is a middle-aged adult managing hypertension and prediabetes with medication and lifestyle changes. He works a high-stress office job and is trying to improve sleep, diet, and exercise. Safety note: allergic to shellfish with moderate reaction risk.

care_preferences.provider_style: Direct and collaborative; prefers clear action steps
care_preferences.care_setting_preference: In-person for annual exams; telehealth for follow-ups
care_preferences.privacy_preference: Share minimum outside primary care team

communication_needs.health_literacy_preference: Balanced — prefers explanations with practical takeaways

active.name: Hypertension
active.notes: Diagnosed 3 years ago; controlled with medication.

active.name: Prediabetes
active.notes: Monitoring diet and activity to prevent progression.

active.name: Obstructive sleep apnea (mild)
active.notes: Uses CPAP intermittently; adherence varies.

past.name: Knee ligament strain
past.notes: Sports-related injury in early 30s; resolved with physical therapy.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Father had hypertension and heart disease. Mother diagnosed with type 2 diabetes in her late 50s. No known hereditary cancer syndromes.

medications.adherence_notes: Generally consistent; occasionally forgets evening dose when working late.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Weight increased slightly over the past year due to reduced activity. Blood pressure improved after medication adjustment.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date on routine vaccines; COVID booster received last year.

specialists.name: Dr. Samuel Ortiz
specialists.role: Sleep apnea evaluation and CPAP management

care_team.pharmacy_preferences: Local pharmacy near workplace for convenience.

behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Trying to reduce sugar and processed foods; prefers home-cooked meals.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Weekend basketball and walking; inconsistent weekday exercise.
behavior_and_lifestyle.stress_management_preferences: Listening to music, short workouts, occasional meditation.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: Recent fatigue and occasional morning headaches; possibly related to inconsistent CPAP use and work stress.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: Monitoring diet and weight; continuing blood pressure medication and metformin.
open_questions_for_provider: Should I adjust CPAP usage or settings to improve sleep quality?
What lifestyle changes would most effectively reverse prediabetes?
Are my morning headaches related to sleep apnea?

critical_alerts: Shellfish allergy — avoid exposure and cross-contamination

profile.baseline_summary: Elena is a young adult managing Type 1 diabetes with insulin and a CGM. She is generally stable but worries about overnight lows and exercise-related glucose swings. Safety note: carries glucagon; history of severe hypoglycemia once in college.

care_preferences.care_setting_preference: Telehealth for routine diabetes follow-ups; in-person annually

active.name: Type 1 diabetes mellitus
active.notes: Diagnosed age 12; uses CGM and insulin pump.

active.name: Generalized anxiety (mild)
active.notes: Stress spikes around glucose variability; uses coping strategies.

past.name: Severe hypoglycemia episode
past.notes: Single event in college; no recurrence in last 5 years.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Maternal aunt with thyroid disease; father with hypertension. No known early cardiac deaths.

medications.adherence_notes: Very adherent; prefers predictable routines and CGM-based adjustments.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Weight stable; glucose control generally steady with occasional exercise-related lows.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date; gets annual flu shot.

care_team.pharmacy_preferences: Mail-order for supplies; local pharmacy for urgent refills.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: No acute symptoms; occasional nighttime alarms and post-exercise lows.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: CGM + pump; adjusting basal rates around workouts.
open_questions_for_provider: How can I reduce overnight low alarms without raising overall glucose?
What’s the best strategy for preventing post-workout lows?
Should I change pump settings during travel?

critical_alerts: Type 1 diabetes — risk of severe hypoglycemia; carries glucagon

profile.baseline_summary: George is an older adult with coronary artery disease and heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, plus chronic kidney disease stage 3. He monitors weight and swelling closely and wants simple medication plans. Safety note: avoid NSAIDs due to kidney function and heart risk.

care_preferences.care_setting_preference: In-person preferred for cardiology; telehealth for brief follow-ups

active.name: Coronary artery disease
active.notes: Stent placed 2018; no recent chest pain.

active.name: Heart failure (HFpEF)
active.notes: Edema with salt; tracks daily weight.

active.name: Chronic kidney disease (stage 3)
active.notes: Stable creatinine; avoids nephrotoxins.

active.name: Hyperlipidemia
active.notes: On statin therapy.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Brother had heart attack in early 60s. Father had hypertension.

medications.adherence_notes: Prefers once-daily meds; occasionally skips diuretic when traveling due to bathroom access.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Weight fluctuates 2–4 lb with salt intake; swelling improves with diuretic adherence.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date; received shingles vaccine series and annual flu shot.

behavior_and_lifestyle.sleep: 6–7 hours; wakes to urinate when diuretic taken late.
behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Low-salt diet; prefers simple meal plans.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Daily walking 20–30 minutes; avoids steep hills when short of breath.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: Occasional ankle swelling after salty meals; mild exertional shortness of breath.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: Diuretic adjustments based on weight; low-salt diet; daily walks.
open_questions_for_provider: When should I adjust my diuretic dose based on weight changes?
What symptoms should prompt urgent evaluation vs routine follow-up?
Are there safer pain relief options given kidney disease?

profile.baseline_summary: Noah is managing major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety with therapy and an SSRI. He is functional at work but has periodic low mood, insomnia, and reduced appetite during high-stress periods. Safety note: history of passive suicidal ideation in the past; currently denies intent but wants a clear plan if symptoms worsen.

communication_needs.health_literacy_preference: Balanced — wants clear next steps without overwhelming details

active.name: Major depressive disorder (recurrent)
active.notes: Responds to SSRI + CBT; flares under work stress.

active.name: Generalized anxiety disorder
active.notes: Rumination and physical tension; benefits from structured coping plan.

active.name: Insomnia (stress-related)
active.notes: Difficulty falling asleep; variable.

past.name: Panic attacks (episodic)
past.notes: More frequent 3 years ago; now rare.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Family history of depression in one parent; no known bipolar disorder.

medications.adherence_notes: Usually adherent; misses doses during travel or disrupted routines.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Weight down ~5 lb during recent stressful month; sleep fragmented.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date; gets flu shot intermittently.

behavior_and_lifestyle.sleep: 5–7 hours; sleep onset delay when anxious.
behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Simple meals; appetite decreases during stress.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Walking and light strength training; inconsistent when mood is low.
behavior_and_lifestyle.stress_management_preferences: Therapy homework, breathing exercises, structured routines.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: Recent increase in rumination, low mood, and insomnia over past 3 weeks.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: Weekly therapy; SSRI daily; sleep hygiene plan in progress.
open_questions_for_provider: Should medication dose be adjusted or should I add a sleep-focused intervention?
What are early warning signs that mean I should seek urgent help?
How can I build a routine that holds during high-stress work periods?

critical_alerts: History of passive suicidal ideation — needs clear escalation plan if symptoms worsen

profile.baseline_summary: Fatima is managing rheumatoid arthritis with a DMARD and intermittent NSAID alternatives. She experiences morning stiffness and flares with stress and poor sleep. Safety note: immunosuppression risk—wants guidance on infection precautions and vaccines.

care_preferences.care_setting_preference: In-person for rheumatology; telehealth for stable follow-ups

accessibility_needs: Occasional mobility support during flares (stairs difficult)

active.name: Rheumatoid arthritis
active.notes: Morning stiffness; periodic flares in hands/wrists.

active.name: Gastritis/GERD (medication-related)
active.notes: Reflux worsens with anti-inflammatory meds.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Mother has osteoarthritis; no known autoimmune diseases reported in first-degree relatives.

medications.adherence_notes: Very adherent; prefers clear written instructions for weekly medication timing.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Weight stable; flare frequency slightly increased during winter.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date; wants confirmation of vaccine timing while on immunosuppression.

recommended: Bone health screening per provider guidance

care_team.pharmacy_preferences: Prefers one pharmacy for all meds to avoid interactions.

behavior_and_lifestyle.sleep: 6–7 hours; pain can disrupt sleep during flares.
behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Mediterranean-style diet; avoids triggers for reflux.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Low-impact exercise (walking, gentle yoga); avoids high-impact during flares.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: Morning hand stiffness ~45 minutes; occasional swelling in wrists; reflux controlled most days.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: Weekly methotrexate with monitoring labs; flare plan with provider.
open_questions_for_provider: How should I adjust activity during a flare vs when stable?
What infection precautions are most important while on methotrexate?
Are there safer pain-control options that won’t worsen reflux?

critical_alerts: On immunosuppressive therapy (methotrexate) — increased infection risk

profile.baseline_summary: Hannah is a young adult with severe IgE-mediated peanut allergy and moderate eczema. She is vigilant about carrying epinephrine and wants a clear plan for travel and dining out. Safety note: high risk of anaphylaxis—do not administer peanut-containing products; confirm cross-contamination precautions.

care_preferences.care_setting_preference: Telehealth for routine allergy follow-up; in-person for testing

active.name: Peanut allergy (anaphylaxis risk)
active.notes: Strict avoidance; carries epinephrine.

active.name: Atopic dermatitis (eczema)
active.notes: Flares with dry weather and stress.

active.name: Allergic rhinitis
active.notes: Seasonal symptoms.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Sibling with seasonal allergies; no known anaphylaxis in family history.

current.name: Epinephrine auto-injector
current.schedule: Carry at all times; use as needed

medications.adherence_notes: Very adherent to carrying epinephrine; prefers the simplest possible daily regimen.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: No major changes; eczema flares more in winter.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Up to date on routine vaccines; interested in confirming any allergy-related contraindications (none known).

due_soon: Epinephrine renewal check and training review (within 2 months)

care_team.pharmacy_preferences: Local pharmacy near home; wants auto-refill reminders for epinephrine expiration.

behavior_and_lifestyle.sleep: 7–8 hours; itch can disrupt sleep during eczema flares.
behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Avoids peanuts and high cross-contamination foods; reads labels carefully.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Pilates and walking; avoids eating before group workouts due to allergy anxiety.
behavior_and_lifestyle.stress_management_preferences: Planning ahead, checklists, and talking with friends.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: No acute allergic symptoms; eczema mild flare on hands.
current_health_context.ongoing_treatments_summary: Strict avoidance; carries epinephrine; topical management for eczema.
open_questions_for_provider: Best practices for dining out and travel to reduce cross-contamination risk?
Do I need two auto-injectors on hand and where should I keep them?
Can eczema control be improved without daily steroids?

critical_alerts: Severe peanut allergy — anaphylaxis risk; epinephrine required if exposure suspected

profile.baseline_summary: Marisol has moderate COPD from past smoking and manages breathlessness with daily inhalers. She has frequent winter bronchitis and wants clear action plans for flare-ups. Safety note: avoid sedating cough medicines that worsen breathing; monitor low oxygen symptoms.

care_preferences.care_setting_preference: In-person for lung checkups; telehealth for quick medication refills

accessibility_needs: Needs breaks when walking long clinic hallways

active.name: COPD (moderate)
active.notes: Former smoker; dyspnea on stairs; uses maintenance inhaler.

active.name: Chronic bronchitis (recurrent)
active.notes: Winter flares; uses action plan.

active.name: Osteopenia
active.notes: Working on weight-bearing exercise and calcium/vitamin D intake.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Mother had osteoporosis; father had lung disease and hypertension.

medications.adherence_notes: Consistent with morning inhaler; sometimes delays rescue inhaler use until symptoms are severe.

vitals_and_measurements.recent_trends_summary: Breathing symptoms slightly worse in winter; activity decreases when weather is cold.

preventive_care.immunizations_summary: Gets annual flu shot; received pneumococcal vaccination per provider.

care_team.pharmacy_preferences: Local pharmacy with Spanish-speaking staff if possible.

behavior_and_lifestyle.sleep: 6–7 hours; cough can wake her during flares.
behavior_and_lifestyle.nutrition_preferences: Tries to limit salty foods; small meals when short of breath.
behavior_and_lifestyle.activity_preferences: Short walks; pulmonary rehab exercises at home.

current_health_context.current_symptoms_summary: More coughing and mild wheeze over the past week; no fever; exertional dyspnea unchanged.

profile.baseline_summary: Omar has ulcerative colitis with periodic flares and has been stable on maintenance therapy for the past year. He is cautious about side effects and wants shared decision-making. Safety note: dehydration risk during flares; needs guidance on when to seek urgent care.

active.name: Ulcerative colitis

active.notes: Occurs during flares; monitored.

medical_history.family_history_summary: Father has type 2 diabetes; no family history of inflammatory bowel disease reported.

medications.adherence_notes: Prefers once-daily meds; adherent when symptoms are present, sometimes less consistent when feeling well.

intolerances.reaction: Worsens GI symptoms during sensitive periods
